input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI workload

```powershell
# Install MAUI (pulls in Android, Windows SDKs automatically)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target needs the Windows App SDK and WinUI. Easiest via VS:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you already have VS, add the workload via CLI:
# Discover your VS install path first:
& "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vswhere.exe" `
  -latest -property installationPath

# Then modify it (adjust path from above):
$vsInstaller = "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe"
& $vsInstaller modify --installPath "<path_from_above>" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet
```

## Step 4 — Set up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs a basic Android SDK, but you may need to manage versions:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was installed
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
  # Fallback: check the dotnet MAUI location
  $androidSdk = "$env:USERPROFILE\.android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# Discover available API levels dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install a specific platform (e.g., the latest you see)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## Step 5 — Set environment variables

```powershell
# Persist ANDROID_HOME
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH for adb
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("PATH", "User")
if ($currentPath -notlike "*platform-tools*") {
  [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("PATH",
    "$currentPath;$androidSdk\platform-tools", "User")
}
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create and build a test project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Discover all available target frameworks in the project
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks" |
  ForEach-Object { $_.Line.Trim() }
```

## Step 8 — Run the app

```powershell
# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (create one first)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" list avd
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd <avd_name> &
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if using .NET 8 — check with `dotnet --list-sdks`.
- The Windows TFM version (`10.0.19041.0`) comes from your `.csproj`; inspect it dynamically with the `Select-String` command in Step 7.
- If `sdkmanager` isn't found, install cmdline-tools: `winget install Google.AndroidSDK`.
